perturbation.original_text: 5.6. Inspection of API. Within thirty (30) calendar days of the arrival of each lot of API at the manufacturing facility designated by INTERSECT, INTERSECT shall inspect and test each lot of API at its own cost and expense. If, upon inspecting and testing the API, INTERSECT determines that a lot of API does not conform to the Product Specifications, then INTERSECT shall, within such thirty (30) day period, give HOVIONE written notice of such non-conformity (setting forth the details of such non-conformity):Unless HOVIONE objects, within 20 working days from the notice by INTERSECT, to the non-conformity INTERSECT will return the non-conforming API to HOVIONE. Any API rejected by INTERSECT may not be reshipped to INTERSECT except if the API is reprocessed according to the DMF. HOVIONE sole responsibility shall be to replace any non-conforming API within thirty (30) days of receiving the notice of non- conformity. Disputes between the Parties as to whether all or any part of a shipment rejected by INTERSECT materially conforms to the Product Specifications shall be resolved by a mutually acceptable third-party testing laboratory located in a neutral country. HOVIONE shall pay all the fees of the third-party laboratory, unless the third-party testing laboratory determines that the delivered API materially conforms to the Product Specifications, in which case INTERSECT shall pay all the fees of such third-party laboratory and also any additional costs that HOVIONE incurred in providing replacement material.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.6. Inspection of API. Within thirty (30) calendar days of the arrival of each lot of API at the manufacturing facility designated by INTERSECT, INTERSECT shall inspect and test each lot of API at its own cost and expense. If, upon inspecting and testing the API, INTERSECT subjectively determines that a lot of API does not meet INTERSECT’s internal quality standards, then INTERSECT shall, within such thirty (30) day period, give HOVIONE written notice of such non-conformity (setting forth the details of such non-conformity):Unless HOVIONE objects, within 20 working days from the notice by INTERSECT, to the non-conformity INTERSECT will return the non-conforming API to HOVIONE. Any API rejected by INTERSECT may not be reshipped to INTERSECT except if the API is reprocessed according to the DMF. HOVIONE sole responsibility shall be to replace any non-conforming API within thirty (30) days of receiving the notice of non- conformity. Disputes between the Parties as to whether all or any part of a shipment rejected by INTERSECT materially conforms to the Product Specifications shall be resolved by a mutually acceptable third-party testing laboratory located in a neutral country. HOVIONE shall pay all the fees of the third-party laboratory, unless the third-party testing laboratory determines that the delivered API materially conforms to the Product Specifications, in which case INTERSECT shall pay all the fees of such third-party laboratory and also any additional costs that HOVIONE incurred in providing replacement material.
perturbation.explanation: The original text requires the API to not conform to the 'Product Specifications', while the modified text allows INTERSECT to reject based on its subjective 'internal quality standards'. This creates a contradiction because, despite the subjectivity, the original text states 'Disputes between the Parties as to whether all or any part of a shipment rejected by INTERSECT materially conforms to the Product Specifications shall be resolved by a mutually acceptable third-party testing laboratory located in a neutral country.' This implies that INTERSECT is entitled to a replacement when it does not conform to the 'Product Specifications', yet the modified text allows for INTERSECT to make a subjective determination. If this subjectively determination is allowed, then the laboratory test may be unnecessary.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.6. Inspection of API. Within thirty (30) calendar days of the arrival of each lot of API at the manufacturing facility designated by INTERSECT, INTERSECT shall inspect and test each lot of API at its own cost and expense. If, upon inspecting and testing the API, INTERSECT subjectively determines that a lot of API does not meet INTERSECT’s internal quality standards, then INTERSECT shall, within such thirty (30) day period, give HOVIONE written notice of such non-conformity (setting forth the details of such non-conformity):Unless HOVIONE objects, within 20 working days from the notice by INTERSECT, to the non-conformity INTERSECT will return the non-conforming API to HOVIONE. Any API rejected by INTERSECT may not be reshipped to INTERSECT except if the API is reprocessed according to the DMF. HOVIONE sole responsibility shall be to replace any non-conforming API within thirty (30) days of receiving the notice of non- conformity. Disputes between the Parties as to whether all or any part of a shipment rejected by INTERSECT materially conforms to the Product Specifications shall be resolved by a mutually acceptable third-party testing laboratory located in a neutral country. HOVIONE shall pay all the fees of the third-party laboratory, unless the third-party testing laboratory determines that the delivered API materially conforms to the Product Specifications, in which case INTERSECT shall pay all the fees of such third-party laboratory and also any additional costs that HOVIONE incurred in providing replacement material.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces the objective 'Product Specifications' with INTERSECT's subjective 'internal quality standards' as the basis for API rejection. This directly contradicts the original intent and enforceability of the clause stating, 'Disputes between the Parties as to whether all or any part of a shipment rejected by INTERSECT materially conforms to the Product Specifications shall be resolved by a mutually acceptable third-party testing laboratory'. The change introduces ambiguity and undermines the purpose of third-party testing, making it unclear under what conditions HOVIONE is obligated to replace the API.

perturbation.original_text: 10.1. Term. Unless terminated in accordance with the provisions of Section 10.2 below, the term of this Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and shall continue in effect for a FIVE (5) year period.
perturbation.changed_text: 10.1. Term. Unless terminated in accordance with the provisions of Section 10.2 below, the term of this Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and shall continue in effect for a minimum of FIVE (5) year period and will automatically extend for subsequent one (1) year periods unless either party notifies the other in writing at least ninety (90) days prior to the expiration of the initial term or any renewal term that it does not wish to renew this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By adding the automatic renewal clause, it creates a contradiction in the term section of the original agreement. The first term is clearly defined as five years, while the altered text provides the possibility of yearly renewals that could create ambiguity on the actual term of the agreement. This creates an ambiguity about when the agreement truly ends.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10.1. Term. Unless terminated in accordance with the provisions of Section 10.2 below, the term of this Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and shall continue in effect for a minimum of FIVE (5) year period and will automatically extend for subsequent one (1) year periods unless either party notifies the other in writing at least ninety (90) days prior to the expiration of the initial term or any renewal term that it does not wish to renew this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the term of the agreement from a fixed five-year period to a term that automatically renews for one-year periods unless either party provides written notice. This creates an ambiguity about the actual term of the agreement, since the original clause clearly states a five (5) year period and the altered text can extend past that period. The added language creates a contradiction, as the agreement could extend significantly beyond five years without any explicit agreement by either party at the conclusion of the initial period, hence it is an example of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.
